title: Psychoacoustics & Timbre Perception

analysis.object: T218: Psychoacoustics and timbre under the Shadow Depth Theorem

loudness_perception.reason: Power law — Stevens' law, log-log linear

timbre_spectral.reason: Weighted sum of harmonics Σ aₙ sin(2πnft+φₙ) = EML-3

attack_decay.description: ADSR envelope: exp decay and exp rise

critical_bands.reason: Discrete count of auditory filter channels

phantom_fundamental.description: Missing fundamental: brain infers f₀ from harmonics
phantom_fundamental.reason: Reconstruction of EML-3 oscillation from its spectral shadows

analysis.sdt_application: SDT applied to timbre: Timbre is the shadow of the harmonic structure at the receiver. shadow(timbre) = shadow(Σ aₙ sin(2πnft)) = shadow(EML-3) = oscillatory. The SDT predicts: human timbre perception must be mediated by an oscillatory mechanism — which is confirmed: auditory cortex uses oscillatory neural populations.

analysis.verdict: Timbre: EML-3. Loudness/pitch: EML-2. ADSR: EML-1. Emotion: EML-∞.
analysis.theorem: T218: Psychoacoustics Depth — timbre is EML-3 shadow; SDT predicts oscillatory perception

key_theorem: T218: Psychoacoustics Depth (S497). Timbre = spectral envelope = EML-3. SDT: shadow(timbre) = oscillatory → brain must use oscillatory mechanism. Confirmed: auditory cortex = oscillatory neural populations. Phantom fundamental: EML-3 reconstruction from spectral shadows.
rabbit_hole_log: Timbre: Σ aₙ sin(2πnft+φₙ) = EML-3 (sum of oscillations)
SDT: shadow of EML-3 = oscillatory → auditory cortex must be oscillatory
Phantom fundamental: EML-3 inferred from EML-3 shadows
T218: SDT makes a testable prediction about auditory cortex architecture
